topic.title: EDI, IDoc & API Integration in SAP TM

learningObjectives.title: Understand integration technologies
learningObjectives.description: Explain EDI, IDoc, XML, and API options used by SAP TM integrations.

learningObjectives.title: Understand document exchange patterns
learningObjectives.description: Describe common inbound and outbound logistics documents in integrated flows.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate IDoc and API usage
learningObjectives.description: Clarify SAP-to-SAP asynchronous exchange versus modern real-time integrations.

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management communicates with external carriers, logistics providers and SAP systems using standard integration technologies such as Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), Intermediate Documents (IDocs), XML messages and REST/OData APIs. These technologies automate business document exchange and eliminate manual communication.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Integration technologies transport business information between SAP TM and external systems—they do not perform transportation planning themselves.

A Freight Order is automatically transmitted to a carrier through an EDI message. The carrier accepts the shipment, sends shipment status updates using APIs and finally transmits Proof of Delivery electronically. SAP TM updates transportation execution without any manual intervention.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Use APIs for modern cloud integrations and IDocs for standard SAP-to-SAP communication wherever appropriate. Follow SAP integration standards to simplify maintenance.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics company integrated SAP TM with more than 150 carriers using APIs and EDI. Shipment status updates became fully automated, reducing manual tracking activities by nearly 80%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: IDoc is traditionally used for SAP system integration, EDI is widely used for B2B partner communication and REST/OData APIs are commonly used for modern integrations.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Integration technologies transfer business information only. Transportation planning, optimization and settlement remain SAP TM business functions.

items: SAP TM integrates using IDocs, EDI, APIs, XML and web services.
IDocs are commonly used for SAP-to-SAP integration.
EDI enables electronic business document exchange with partners.
REST/OData APIs support modern real-time integrations.
Automated integration improves transportation efficiency and data accuracy.
